# =============================================================================
# RTMDet fine-tuning configuration
# Edit this file, then run:
#   Step 1 — python normalize_dataset.py
#   Step 2 — python finetune_rtmdet.py
# =============================================================================

[dataset]
# Absolute path to your dataset folder.
# Use the _rtmdet version generated by prepare_yolo_dataset_for_rtmdet.py,
# or point directly to your YOLO dataset (train/valid/test + data.yaml).
dataset_path = C:\Users\AntonioreneèBarletta\Desktop\train_RTM_det\datasets\Fire-Smoke-Detection_rtmdet

# Optional: comma-separated class names. Leave empty to read from data.yaml.
# Example: class_names = Fire, Smoke
class_names =

# Optional: number of classes. Leave empty to read from data.yaml.
nc =


[model]
# Name used for the run folder and the output package.
model_name = rtmdet_fire_flame_640_s

# RTMDet variant: s (small, faster) or m (medium, more accurate).
variant = s

# Input image size in pixels. Must be a multiple of 32.
# TensorRT export is currently static at this value.
imgsz = 640

# Path to a custom pretrained checkpoint (.pth).
# Leave empty to download the official COCO checkpoint for the chosen variant.
pretrained_checkpoint =


[training]
epochs          = 200
stage2_epochs   = 20
batch_size      = 32
val_batch_size  = 8
workers         = 8
val_interval    = 5
base_lr         = 0.001
device          = cuda:0
seed            = 1

# Automatic Mixed Precision (recommended: True on modern GPUs).
amp    = True

# Set True to resume from the last checkpoint in project_dir/model_name/.
resume = False

# Log progress every N iterations.
logger_interval = 100


[pipeline]
# True  → validate dataset and generate config only; do NOT start training.
# False → run the full pipeline according to the flags below.
prepare_only   = False

run_training   = True
run_evaluation = False

# Export the best checkpoint to ONNX after training.
# Requires mmdeploy_root to be set in [paths].
# NMS thresholds and output size are configured in [onnx_export] below.
save_onnx_weights = True

run_packaging  = True


[onnx_export]
# Score threshold baked into the ONNX graph.
# Detections with confidence below this value are discarded before NMS.
# Cannot be lowered at inference without re-exporting.
# Recommended for fire/smoke: 0.05 (light filter, decide at inference time).
score_threshold = 0.05

# IoU threshold for Non-Maximum Suppression baked into the graph.
# Lower → more aggressive deduplication.  Higher → more overlapping boxes.
iou_threshold = 0.5

# Maximum number of detections per image (defines the fixed output tensor size).
# Unused slots are zero-padded. 300 covers any realistic scenario.
keep_top_k = 10


[paths]
# Leave empty to use defaults relative to the current working directory:
#   project_dir → <cwd>/runs/rtmdet
#   package_dir → <cwd>/models/rtmdet
project_dir =
package_dir =

# Absolute path to your MMDetection clone (the folder containing tools/train.py).
# REQUIRED for training. Example:
#   mmdet_root = C:\Users\name\Desktop\mmdetection
mmdet_root = C:\Users\AntonioreneèBarletta\Desktop\train_RTM_det\mmdetection

# Absolute path to your MMDeploy clone. Only needed when save_onnx_weights = True.
# Example:
#   mmdeploy_root = C:\Users\name\Desktop\mmdeploy
mmdeploy_root = C:\Users\AntonioreneèBarletta\Desktop\train_RTM_det\mmdeploy


[preprocessing]
# Rename dataset files to a canonical format before training.
# Runs automatically in normalize_dataset.py; keep False here unless you
# also want the pipeline to re-normalize on every run.
normalize_names = False

# Convert YOLO segmentation annotations to bounding boxes automatically.
convert_segments_to_boxes = True

# Allow images without a corresponding .txt label (treated as negatives).
allow_missing_labels = False

# Stop the pipeline if dataset validation finds errors.
stop_on_validation_errors = True
